findings.message: New `redis` scrape job targets `redis-exporter:9121`, but no `redis-exporter` service is defined in any Compose file (only `postgres-exporter` and `otel-collector` exist in infra/compose/observability.yml). The scrape target has no corresponding exporter, so Prometheus will permanently fail to scrape this job (target DOWN). The self-hosted Redis data store in infra/compose/services.yml is not instrumented.
findings.suggestion: Add a `redis-exporter` service (e.g. oliver006/redis_exporter) to infra/compose/observability.yml wired to the Redis instance, or remove this scrape job if Redis metrics are not yet exported.

findings.message: New `redis` Prometheus scrape job is added with no matching alert rule. Every other scraped surface has a dedicated alert file (postgres_alerts.yml, prometheus_alerts.yml, otel_collector_alerts.yml, slo_alerts.yml), but there is no redis_alerts.yml under infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/. Redis can fail (e.g. exporter down, memory pressure, replication break) with no alert firing.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml with at least an exporter-up / RedisDown alert and a memory/eviction alert, matching the pattern of the other surfaces.

findings.message: New `redis` scrape job has no corresponding Grafana dashboard. The dashboards directory (infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/) contains postgres.json, prometheus.json, otel-collector.json, and slo.json, but no redis.json, so the scraped Redis metrics are not visualized for any operator.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json with the key Redis panels (memory, hit rate, connected clients, ops/sec) to match the per-surface dashboard pattern.
